title: Three-term triangle inequality for real absolute value
informal_statement: For every triple of real numbers a, b, c: |a + b + c| <= |a| + |b| + |c| (the three-term triangle inequality for real absolute value).
informal_proof_idea: Case split on the sign of each of a, b, c, and a+b+c via abs_cases (16 branches total), which replaces each absolute value with its signed unfolding; nlinarith closes every resulting purely-arithmetic branch uniformly.
formal_statement_pp: theorem abs_add_three (a b c : ℝ) : |a + b + c| ≤ |a| + |b| + |c|

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 00c6c1c7), kernel_verified on the second Solve attempt in this episode (the first tried `linarith [abs_add (a + b) c, abs_add a b]`, citing a lemma name (`abs_add`) that does not exist under this pinned Mathlib rev; switched to the same `abs_cases`-based case-split technique already used by the paired abs_add_le packet, which is more robust to exact-lemma-name drift). A separate, fully broken problem_version attempt (missing `Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith` in `problem_imports`) was abandoned via `give_up` before this one. Natural extension of elementary.inequalities.abs_add_le.v1 to three terms; deliberately picked to continue offsetting this domain's L0_elementary/L2_olympiad skew.
